mnemonics: Think of CARTesian Products as Creating A Real Table. The first set forms the rows, the second set forms the columns, and each cell is an ordered pair (x, y).
keyPoints: A Cartesian Product A × B is the set of all possible ordered pairs (a, b) where a ∈ A and b ∈ B.
A relation R from set A to set B is simply a subset of the Cartesian Product A × B.
A function is a special type of relation where every element in the domain (A) is related to exactly one element in the codomain (B).
commonMistakes: Confusing the order in an ordered pair: (a, b) is not the same as (b, a) unless a = b.
Assuming all relations are functions. A relation where an element in A is related to multiple elements in B is not a function.
Forgetting that the Cartesian Product of two sets results in a set of pairs, not a set of individual elements.
